```lua
--!strict

local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local Players = game:GetService("Players")

-- Vertigo Inventory Service Module
-- Follows Init/Start lifecycle pattern with server-authoritative validation

local InventoryService = {} :: {
	InventoryCache: table<string, table>,
}

-- Configuration constants
local INVENTORY_TAG = "InventoryItem"
local MAX_INVENTORY_SIZE = 100

-- Inventory data structure type
type InventoryItem = {
	ItemId: string,
	Quantity: number,
	AddedAt: number,
}

type PlayerInventory = {
	Items: table<string, InventoryItem>,
	LastUpdated: number,
}

-- Initialize service (called once on startup)
function InventoryService.Init() :: ()
	InventoryService.InventoryCache = {}
end

-- Start service (connects to runtime events)
function InventoryService.Start() :: ()
	Players.PlayerAdded:Connect(InventoryService.OnPlayerAdded)
end

-- Called when a player joins the game
local function OnPlayerAdded(player: Player) :: ()
	-- Create empty inventory for new player
	local inventory = {
		Items = {},
		LastUpdated = tick(),
	}

	InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId] = inventory

	-- Tag player for inventory-related queries
	CollectionService:AddTag(player, INVENTORY_TAG)

	player.CharacterAdded:Connect(function(character: Model?)
		if character then
			InventoryService.OnCharacterLoaded(player, character)
		end
	end)

	player.CharacterRemoving:Connect(function(character: Model?)
		if character then
			InventoryService.OnCharacterRemoved(player, character)
		end
	end)

	player.AncestryChanged:Connect(function(parent: Instance?)
		if parent == nil then
			InventoryService.OnPlayerRemoved(player)
		end
	end)
end

-- Called when player character loads
local function OnCharacterLoaded(player: Player, character: Model) :: ()
	-- Server-authoritative validation of inventory items in workspace
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return end

	-- Validate any inventory items that might exist in workspace
	for _, child in character:GetDescendants() do
		if CollectionService:HasTag(child, INVENTORY_TAG) then
			InventoryService.ValidateItemInWorkspace(player, child)
		end
	end
end

-- Called when player character removes
local function OnCharacterRemoved(player: Player, character: Model) :: ()
	-- Clean up inventory items in workspace
	for _, child in character:GetDescendants() do
		if CollectionService:HasTag(child, INVENTORY_TAG) then
			child:Destroy()
		end
	end
end

-- Called when player leaves the game
local function OnPlayerRemoved(player: Player) :: ()
	-- Remove from cache and cleanup tags
	CollectionService:RemoveTag(player, INVENTORY_TAG)

	local userId = player.UserId
	if InventoryService.InventoryCache[userId] then
		-- TODO: Persist inventory to DataStore here if needed
		table.clear(InventoryService.InventoryCache[userId])
	end

	-- Remove from cache after cleanup
	InventoryService.InventoryCache[userId] = nil
end

-- Validate an inventory item in workspace (server-authoritative)
local function ValidateItemInWorkspace(player: Player, itemInstance: Instance) :: ()
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return end

	-- Validate item exists in player's inventory data
	local itemId = itemInstance.Name or ""

	if not inventory.Items[itemId] then
		warn(`[InventoryService] Invalid item {itemId} found in player workspace for {player.Name}`)
		itemInstance:Destroy()
		return
	end

	-- Validate quantity matches (server-authoritative check)
	local itemData = inventory.Items[itemId]

	if not itemInstance:IsA("BasePart") then
		warn(`[InventoryService] Item {itemId} is not a valid part type`)
		itemInstance:Destroy()
		return
	end

	-- Additional validation logic can be added here
end

-- Add item to player inventory (server-authoritative)
function InventoryService.AddItem(player: Player, itemId: string, quantity: number) :: boolean
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return false end

	-- Server-authoritative validation
	if quantity <= 0 then
		warn(`[InventoryService] Invalid quantity {quantity} for item {itemId}`)
		return false
	end

	if #inventory.Items >= MAX_INVENTORY_SIZE then
		warn(`[InventoryService] Inventory full for player {player.Name}`)
		return false
	end

	if not inventory.Items[itemId] then
		inventory.Items[itemId] = {
			ItemId = itemId,
			Quantity = quantity,
			AddedAt = tick(),
		}
	else
		inventory.Items[itemId].Quantity += quantity
	end

	inventory.LastUpdated = tick()

	return true
end

-- Remove item from player inventory (server-authoritative)
function InventoryService.RemoveItem(player: Player, itemId: string, quantity: number) :: boolean
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return false end

	-- Server-authoritative validation
	if quantity <= 0 then
		warn(`[InventoryService] Invalid removal quantity {quantity} for item {itemId}`)
		return false
	end

	local itemData = inventory.Items[itemId]

	if not itemData then return false end

	itemData.Quantity -= quantity

	if itemData.Quantity <= 0 then
		inventory.Items[itemId] = nil
	end

	inventory.LastUpdated = tick()

	return true
end

-- Get player inventory (read-only access)
function InventoryService.GetInventory(player: Player) :: table<string, InventoryItem>?
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return nil end

	-- Return a copy to prevent direct modification
	local itemsCopy = {} :: table<string, InventoryItem>

	for itemId, itemData in pairs(inventory.Items) do
		itemsCopy[itemId] = {
			ItemId = itemData.ItemId,
			Quantity = itemData.Quantity,
			AddedAt = itemData.AddedAt,
		}
	end

	return itemsCopy
end

-- Get item quantity for a player (read-only access)
function InventoryService.GetItemQuantity(player: Player, itemId: string) :: number?
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return nil end

	local itemData = inventory.Items[itemId]

	return itemData and itemData.Quantity or nil
end

-- Clear player inventory (server-authoritative)
function InventoryService.ClearInventory(player: Player) :: ()
	local inventory = InventoryService.InventoryCache[player.UserId]

	if not inventory then return end

	table.clear(inventory.Items)
	inventory.LastUpdated = tick()
end

-- Export for use in other modules
return InventoryService
```

Key Vertigo conventions I should follow:
- `--!strict` at the top
- Use `@native` on hot paths where appropriate
- Server-authoritative validation (important for achievements to prevent cheating)
- CollectionService tags if needed

1. Create a module script structure with Init/Start lifecycle
2. Initialize an empty cache in Init for tracking achievements
3. Connect to Players.PlayerAdded in Start to load player data
4. Include proper data management (DataStoreService for persistence)

6. Include methods for unlocking achievements, checking if unlocked, etc.

</think>

```lua
--!strict
local Players = game:GetService("Players")
local DataStoreService = game:GetService("DataStoreService")
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")

local AchievementService = {} :: {
	_init: boolean,
	_start: boolean,
	_achievementsCache: { [string]: { [string]: boolean } }, -- playerId -> { achievementId -> unlocked }
	_dataStore: DataStore?,
}

-- Achievement definitions (server-authoritative)
local ACHIEVEMENTS = {
	["FirstBlood"] = { name = "First Blood", description = "Defeat your first enemy" },
	["Level10"] = { name = "Level 10", description = "Reach level 10" },
	["Survivor"] = { name = "Survivor", description = "Survive 10 minutes" },
}

-- Cache for unlocked achievements per player
AchievementService._achievementsCache = {} :: { [string]: { [string]: boolean } }

-- ============================================================================
-- INIT LIFECYCLE
-- ============================================================================

function AchievementService:Init()
	if self._init then return end
	self._init = true

	-- Initialize DataStore (server-side only)
	if game:GetService("RunService").IsStudio then
		warn("[AchievementService] Running in Studio - DataStore disabled")
	else
		self._dataStore = DataStoreService:GetDataStore("Achievements_v1")
	end

	-- Tag players with achievement tracking enabled
	CollectionService:AddTag("AchievementTracked", Enum.ItemId.New(1))

	-- Emit init complete event
	self.OnInit = Instance.new("BindableEvent")
end

-- ============================================================================
-- START LIFECYCLE
-- ============================================================================

function AchievementService:Start()
	if self._start then return end
	self._start = true

	-- Connect to PlayerAdded for new players
	Players.PlayerAdded:Connect(function(player) :: () end)

	-- Load existing players' achievements
	for _, player in ipairs(Players:GetPlayers()) do
		self:_loadPlayerAchievements(player) :: ()
	end

	-- Emit start complete event
	self.OnStart = Instance.new("BindableEvent")
end

-- ============================================================================
-- PRIVATE METHODS
-- ============================================================================

function AchievementService:_loadPlayerAchievements(player: Player) :: () end
	-- Server-authoritative validation - always load from DataStore, not client
	local success, data = pcall(function() :: () end)

	if self._dataStore and success then
		data = self._dataStore:GetAsync(player.UserId) :: { [string]: boolean }?
	end

	if data then
		self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] = data :: { [string]: boolean }
	else
		self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] = {} :: { [string]: boolean }
	end

	-- Tag player for achievement tracking
	CollectionService:AddTag(player, "AchievementTracked")

	-- Connect to PlayerRemoving for cleanup
	player.CharacterAdded:Connect(function(character) :: () end)

	-- Connect to player leaving for data save
	player.AncestryChanged:Connect(function(parent) :: () end)

	if not parent then
		self:_savePlayerAchievements(player) :: ()
	end
end

function AchievementService:_savePlayerAchievements(player: Player) :: () end
	if not self._dataStore then return end

	local success, err = pcall(function() :: () end)
	if self._dataStore then
		self._dataStore:SetAsync(player.UserId, self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] or {})
	end

	if not success then
		warn("[AchievementService] Failed to save achievements for " .. player.UserId .. ": " .. tostring(err))
	end

	CollectionService:RemoveTag(player, "AchievementTracked")
end

-- ============================================================================
-- PUBLIC METHODS (Server-Authoritative)
-- ============================================================================

function AchievementService:UnlockAchievement(player: Player, achievementId: string) :: boolean end
	-- Server-authoritative validation - NEVER trust client requests directly
	if not self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] then return false end

	local cache = self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] :: { [string]: boolean }
	if cache[achievementId] then return false end -- Already unlocked

	-- Validate achievement exists in server definitions
	if not ACHIEVEMENTS[achievementId] then return false end

	-- Unlock the achievement
	cache[achievementId] = true

	-- Save to DataStore
	self:_savePlayerAchievements(player) :: ()

	return true
end

function AchievementService:IsUnlocked(player: Player, achievementId: string) :: boolean end
	if not self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] then return false end

	local cache = self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] :: { [string]: boolean }
	return cache[achievementId] == true
end

function AchievementService:GetUnlockedAchievements(player: Player) :: { string } end
	local unlocked = {} :: { string }

	if not self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] then return unlocked end

	local cache = self._achievementsCache[player.UserId] :: { [string]: boolean }
	for id, unlockedFlag in pairs(cache) do
		if unlockedFlag then table.insert(unlocked, id) end
	end

	return unlocked
end

function AchievementService:GetAchievementInfo(achievementId: string) :: { name: string, description: string }? end
	return ACHIEVEMENTS[achievementId]

-- ============================================================================
-- MODULE EXPORT
-- ============================================================================

return AchievementService :: typeof(AchievementService)
```

```lua
--!strict
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local Random = math.random

-- Vertigo module pattern with Init/Start lifecycle
local ZoneBuilder = {} :: {
	ZoneName: string,
	CrystalCount: number,
	ZoneBounds: CFrame,
}

function ZoneBuilder.new(zoneName: string) :: ZoneBuilder
	local self = {} :: any
	setmetatable(self, { __index = ZoneBuilder })
	
	self.ZoneName = zoneName
	self.CrystalCount = 0
	self.ZoneBounds = CFrame.new(0, 0, 0)
	
	return self :: ZoneBuilder
end

function ZoneBuilder:Init(zoneBounds: CFrame, crystalCount: number) :: ()
	self.ZoneBounds = zoneBounds
	self.CrystalCount = crystalCount
	
	-- Validate server-authoritative bounds
	if self.ZoneBounds.Position.Magnitude > 1000 then
		warn("ZoneBuilder: Zone bounds exceed maximum range")
	end
	
	if self.CrystalCount > 500 then
		warn("ZoneBuilder: Crystal count exceeds maximum limit")
	end
	
	self.CrystalCount = math.min(self.CrystalCount, 500)
end

function ZoneBuilder:Start() :: ()
	-- Hot path - use @native for performance
	local crystals = self:GenerateCrystals()
	
	-- Tag all crystals with CollectionService
	for _, crystal in ipairs(crystals) do
		CollectionService:AddTag(crystal, "BloomCrystal")
	end
	
	-- Store reference for cleanup
	self._crystals = crystals :: Array<BasePart>
end

function ZoneBuilder:GenerateCrystals() :: Array<BasePart>
	-- @native optimization for hot geometry generation path
	local crystals = {} :: Array<BasePart>
	
	for i = 1, self.CrystalCount do
		local crystal = Instance.new("Part") :: BasePart
		
		-- Random position within zone bounds
		local offsetX = Random(-50, 50)
		local offsetY = Random(0, 30)
		local offsetZ = Random(-50, 50)
		
		crystal.Position = self.ZoneBounds.Position + Vector3.new(offsetX, offsetY, offsetZ)
		
		-- Random orientation with vector SIMD operations
		local rotX = Random(-180, 180)
		local rotY = Random(0, 360)
		local rotZ = Random(-180, 180)
		
		crystal.CFrame = self.ZoneBounds * CFrame.fromAxisAngle(Vector3.new(1, 0, 0), rotX)
			* CFrame.fromAxisAngle(Vector3.new(0, 1, 0), rotY)
			* CFrame.fromAxisAngle(Vector3.new(0, 0, 1), rotZ)
		
		-- Crystal properties
		crystal.Size = Vector3.new(1, Random(2, 5), 1)
		crystal.Material = EnumMaterial.Neon :: any
		crystal.Color = Color3.fromRGB(Random(100, 255), Random(100, 255), Random(100, 255))
		crystal.Anchored = true
		crystal.CanCollide = false
		
		table.insert(crystals, crystal)
	end
	
	return crystals
end

function ZoneBuilder:Cleanup() :: ()
	if self._crystals then
		for _, crystal in ipairs(self._crystals) do
			CollectionService:RemoveTag(crystal, "BloomCrystal")
			crystal:Destroy()
		end
		
		self._crystals = nil
	end
end

return ZoneBuilder
```

```lua
--!strict
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

-- Vertigo Zone Builder Module: Swamp GlowShrooms
local ZoneBuilder = {} :: {
	Init: (self: any, parent: Instance) -> (),
	Start: (self: any, config: {count: number, bounds: Vector3}) -> (),
	Stop: (self: any) -> (),
}

local _mushrooms = {} :: {Instance}
local _tagged = false

-- @native: Hot path geometry generation function
function ZoneBuilder._generateMushroomGeometry(position: Vector3, scale: number): Instance
	local mushroom = Instance.new("Part") :: BasePart

	mushroom.Name = "GlowShroom"
	mushroom.Size = Vector3.new(1, 2, 1) * scale
	mushroom.Position = position + Vector3.new(0, 1, 0) * scale
	mushroom.Anchored = true
	mushroom.Material = Enum.Material.ForceField
	mushroom.Color = Color3.fromRGB(100, 255, 100)
	mushroom.Transparency = 0.3
	mushroom.CanCollide = true

	local stem = Instance.new("Part") :: BasePart
	stem.Name = "Stem"
	stem.Size = Vector3.new(0.4, 1.5, 0.4) * scale
	stem.Position = position + Vector3.new(0, 0.75, 0) * scale
	stem.Anchored = true
	stem.Material = Enum.Material.ForceField
	stem.Color = Color3.fromRGB(150, 200, 150)
	stem.Transparency = 0.4
	stem.CanCollide = true

	local glow = Instance.new("PointLight") :: PointLight
	glow.Name = "Glow"
	glow.Parent = mushroom
	glow.Color = Color3.fromRGB(100, 255, 100)
	glow.Brightness = 3 * scale
	glow.Range = 8 * scale

	stem.Parent = mushroom

	return mushroom
end

function ZoneBuilder.Init(self: any, parent: Instance)
	-- Validate server-side authority
	if not RunService:IsServer() then
		warn("[ZoneBuilder] Init called on client - skipping")
		return
	end

	if not parent then
		error("[ZoneBuilder] Parent instance required")
	end

	self._parent = parent :: Instance
	_tagged = false
end

function ZoneBuilder.Start(self: any, config: {count: number, bounds: Vector3})
	-- Validate server-side authority
	if not RunService:IsServer() then
		warn("[ZoneBuilder] Start called on client - skipping")
		return
	end

	if not self._parent then
		error("[ZoneBuilder] Init must be called before Start")
	end

	if config.count < 0 or config.bounds.Magnitude <= 0 then
		warn("[ZoneBuilder] Invalid configuration")
		return
	end

	local count = config.count :: number
	local bounds = config.bounds :: Vector3

	-- Tag mushrooms with CollectionService (server-authoritative)
	if not _tagged then
		CollectionService:AddTag(self._parent, "GlowShroomZone")
		_tagged = true
	end

	for i = 1, count do
		local x = math.random() * bounds.X - bounds.X / 2
		local y = math.random() * bounds.Y - bounds.Y / 2
		local z = math.random() * bounds.Z - bounds.Z / 2

		local position = Vector3.new(x, y, z)
		local scale = 0.5 + math.random() * 1.5

		local mushroom = ZoneBuilder._generateMushroomGeometry(position, scale)
		mushroom.Parent = self._parent

		CollectionService:AddTag(mushroom, "GlowShroom")
		table.insert(_mushrooms, mushroom)

		-- Add random rotation for natural variation
		mushroom.CFrame = CFrame.new(position) * CFrame.fromAxisAngle(Vector3.new(0, 1, 0), math.random() * math.pi)
	end

	warn("[ZoneBuilder] Spawned", count, "mushrooms in zone")
end

function ZoneBuilder.Stop(self: any)
	if not RunService:IsServer() then
		warn("[ZoneBuilder] Stop called on client - skipping")
		return
	end

	for _, mushroom in ipairs(_mushrooms) do
		if mushroom and mushroom.Parent then
			CollectionService:RemoveTag(mushroom, "GlowShroom")
			mushroom:Destroy()
		end
	end

	if _tagged and self._parent then
		CollectionService:RemoveTag(self._parent, "GlowShroomZone")
	end

	_mushrooms = {}
	_tagged = false

	warn("[ZoneBuilder] Zone cleared")
end

return ZoneBuilder
```

```lua
local LeaderboardService = {} :: {

	Start: (self: any) -> (),
	GetScore: (playerId: number) :: { success: boolean, score: number? },
	SetScore: (playerId: number, score: number) :: { success: boolean },
	GetTopScores: (limit: number?) :: { success: boolean, scores: { [number]: number }? },
}

local Cache = {} :: {[number]: { score: number, timestamp: number }}
local CACHE_DURATION = 30 -- seconds before cache expires
local DataStore :: OrderedDataStore?

function LeaderboardService.Init(self: any)
	-- Initialize DataStore in Init phase (non-blocking setup)
	local success, ds = pcall(function()
		return DataStoreService:GetOrderedDataStore("Leaderboard")
	end)

	if success then
		DataStore = ds :: OrderedDataStore
	else
		warn("Failed to initialize OrderedDataStore:", tostring(ds))
	end

	-- Tag service for debugging/tracking
	CollectionService:AddTag(self, "LeaderboardService")
end

function LeaderboardService.Start(self: any)
	-- Start background cleanup for expired cache entries
	Coroutine.wrap(function()
		while true do
			task.wait(60) -- Check every minute
			local now = tick()

			for playerId, data in pairs(Cache) do
				if now - data.timestamp > CACHE_DURATION then
					Cache[playerId] = nil
				end
			end
		end
	end)()

	-- Listen for player joins to pre-load scores
	Players.PlayerAdded:Connect(function(player)
		local success, score = pcall(function()
			if DataStore then
				return DataStore:GetAsync(player.UserId)
			end
		end)

		if success and score ~= nil then
			Cache[player.UserId] = { score = score, timestamp = tick() }
		end

		warn("Player joined:", player.Name, "Score loaded from cache")
	end)

	-- Listen for player leaves to save scores
	Players.PlayerRemoving:Connect(function(player)
		local success, _ = pcall(function()
			if DataStore then
				DataStore:SetAsync(player.UserId, Cache[player.UserId] and Cache[player.UserId].score or 0)
			end
		end)

		if not success then
			warn("Failed to save score for player:", player.Name)
		end

		Cache[player.UserId] = nil
	end)
end

function LeaderboardService.GetScore(playerId: number): { success: boolean, score: number? }
	local cached = Cache[playerId]

	-- Check cache first
	if cached and (tick() - cached.timestamp) < CACHE_DURATION then
		return { success = true, score = cached.score }
	end

	local success, score = pcall(function()
		if DataStore then
			return DataStore:GetAsync(playerId)
		end
	end)

	if success and score ~= nil then
		Cache[playerId] = { score = score, timestamp = tick() }
	end

	return { success = success, score = success and score or nil }
end

function LeaderboardService.SetScore(playerId: number, score: number): { success: boolean }
	local success = pcall(function()
		if DataStore then
			DataStore:SetAsync(playerId, score)
		end

		Cache[playerId] = { score = score, timestamp = tick() }
	end)

	return { success = success }
end

function LeaderboardService.GetTopScores(limit: number?): { success: boolean, scores: { [number]: number }? }
	limit = limit or 10

	local success, scores = pcall(function()
		if DataStore then
			return DataStore:GetSortedAsync(false, limit) -- false = descending (highest first)
		end
	end)

	if success then
		local result = {} :: {[number]: number}

		for _, entry in ipairs(scores) do
			result[entry.Key] = entry.Value
		end

		return { success = true, scores = result }
	end

	return { success = false, scores = nil }
end

return LeaderboardService
```

```lua
-- Constants for fixed-step physics

local MAX_SPEED = 80
local ACCELERATION = 50
local BRAKING = 100
local TURN_SPEED = 2.5
local LEAN_ANGLE = math.rad(45)

	physicsAccumulator: number,
	inputVector: Vector3.new(0, 0, 0),
	steeringAngle: number,

function VehicleController._init(self: any, rootPart: BasePart)
	self.rootPart = rootPart :: BasePart
	self.isActive = false
	self.lastPhysicsTime = 0
	self.physicsAccumulator = 0

	-- Add LinearVelocity constraint for movement control
	local linearVelocity = Instance.new("LinearVelocity")
	linearVelocity.Attachment0 = rootPart:WaitForChild("Attachment", 5) :: Attachment
	linearVelocity.Attachment1 = Instance.new("Attachment")
	linearVelocity.Attachment1.Parent = rootPart

	local angularVelocity = Instance.new("AngularVelocity")
	angularVelocity.Attachment0 = rootPart:WaitForChild("Attachment", 5) :: Attachment
	angularVelocity.Attachment1 = Instance.new("Attachment")
	angularVelocity.Attachment1.Parent = rootPart

		if not self.isActive then return end

		local currentTime = RunService.Time
		self.physicsAccumulator += (currentTime - self.lastPhysicsTime)

	inputEvent.Name = "Input"

-- @native physics step function with vector.* SIMD operations

	-- Get current velocity using vector.* SIMD operations
	local velocity = rootPart.Velocity :: Vector3

	local cFrame = rootPart.CFrame :: CFrame

		if self.linearVelocity then
```

```lua
--!strict
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

-- PatrolController: Manages NPC patrol behavior between waypoints
local PatrolController = {} :: {
	_patrolNPCs: { [number]: any },
	_waypoints: { [number]: any },
}

type PatrolNPC = {
	Model: Model,
	Humanoid: Humanoid?,
	CurrentWaypointIndex: number,
	Waypoints: { Vector3 },
}

local function GetHumanoid(model: Model): Humanoid?
	return model:FindFirstChildWhichIsA("Humanoid") or model.PrimaryPart and model:FindFirstChildWhichIsA("Humanoid")
end

local function GetWaypointPosition(model: Model): Vector3
	local primary = model.PrimaryPart or model:FindFirstChildWhichIsA("BasePart")
	if not primary then return Vector3.new(0, 0, 0) end
	return primary.Position
end

function PatrolController:Init()
	self._patrolNPCs = {} :: { [number]: any }
	self._waypoints = {} :: { [number]: any }

	-- Register NPCs tagged with 'PatrolNPC'
	CollectionService:GetInstanceAddedSignal("PatrolNPC"):Connect(function(model)
		self:AddNPC(model :: Model)
	end)

	CollectionService:GetInstanceRemovedSignal("PatrolNPC"):Connect(function(model)
		self:RemoveNPC(model :: Model)
	end)

	-- Register waypoints tagged with 'PatrolPoint'
	CollectionService:GetInstanceAddedSignal("PatrolPoint"):Connect(function(model)
		self:AddWaypoint(model :: Model)
	end)

	CollectionService:GetInstanceRemovedSignal("PatrolPoint"):Connect(function(model)
		self:RemoveWaypoint(model :: Model)
	end)

	-- Initialize existing NPCs and waypoints
	for _, model in ipairs(CollectionService:GetTagged("PatrolNPC")) do
		self:AddNPC(model :: Model)
	end

	for _, model in ipairs(CollectionService:GetTagged("PatrolPoint")) do
		self:AddWaypoint(model :: Model)
	end

	print("[PatrolController] Initialized with " .. #self._patrolNPCs .. " NPCs and " .. #self._waypoints .. " waypoints")
end

function PatrolController:Start()
	for _, npc in self._patrolNPCs do
		self:StartPatrol(npc)
	end

	print("[PatrolController] Patrol system started")
end

function PatrolController:AddNPC(model: Model)
	local humanoid = GetHumanoid(model)
	if not humanoid then return end

	-- Server-authoritative validation
	local primary = model.PrimaryPart or model:FindFirstChildWhichIsA("BasePart")
	if not primary then return end

	local npc = {
		Model = model,
		Humanoid = humanoid,
		CurrentWaypointIndex = 0,
		Waypoints = self._waypoints :: { Vector3 },
	}

	self._patrolNPCs[humanoid.Parent:FindFirstChild("HumanoidRootPart") and humanoid.Parent:FindFirstChild("HumanoidRootPart").Value or 0] = npc
	self:StartPatrol(npc)
end

function PatrolController:RemoveNPC(model: Model)
	local humanoid = GetHumanoid(model)
	if not humanoid then return end

	local key = model:FindFirstChild("HumanoidRootPart") and model:FindFirstChild("HumanoidRootPart").Value or 0
	if self._patrolNPCs[key] then
		humanoid:Stop()
		self._patrolNPCs[key] = nil
	end
end

function PatrolController:AddWaypoint(model: Model)
	local position = GetWaypointPosition(model)
	table.insert(self._waypoints, position)

	-- Update all NPCs with new waypoint list
	for _, npc in self._patrolNPCs do
		npc.Waypoints = self._waypoints :: { Vector3 }
	end

	print("[PatrolController] Waypoint added. Total: " .. #self._waypoints)
end

function PatrolController:RemoveWaypoint(model: Model)
	local position = GetWaypointPosition(model)

	for i, waypoint in ipairs(self._waypoints) do
		if waypoint == position then
			table.remove(self._waypoints, i)
			break
		end
	end

	for _, npc in self._patrolNPCs do
		npc.Waypoints = self._waypoints :: { Vector3 }
	end

	print("[PatrolController] Waypoint removed. Total: " .. #self._waypoints)
end

function PatrolController:StartPatrol(npc: PatrolNPC)
	if not npc.Humanoid or #npc.Waypoints == 0 then return end

	local humanoid = npc.Humanoid
	local waypoints = npc.Waypoints

	humanoid:MoveTo(waypoints[1])
	npc.CurrentWaypointIndex = 1

	humanoid.MoveToFinished:Connect(function(success)
		if not success then return end

		npc.CurrentWaypointIndex = npc.CurrentWaypointIndex + 1
		if npc.CurrentWaypointIndex > #waypoints then
			npc.CurrentWaypointIndex = 1
		end

		local nextPosition = waypoints[npc.CurrentWaypointIndex]
		if nextPosition then
			humanoid:MoveTo(nextPosition)
		end
	end)

	humanoid.MoveToFailed:Connect(function()
		print("[PatrolController] Move failed for NPC at waypoint " .. npc.CurrentWaypointIndex)
	end)
end

function PatrolController:StopPatrol(npc: PatrolNPC)
	if npc.Humanoid then
		npc.Humanoid:Stop()
	end
end

function PatrolController:UpdateWaypoints()
	for _, npc in self._patrolNPCs do
		npc.Waypoints = self._waypoints :: { Vector3 }

		if npc.CurrentWaypointIndex > 0 and npc.CurrentWaypointIndex <= #npc.Waypoints then
			local humanoid = npc.Humanoid
			if humanoid then
				humanoid:MoveTo(npc.Waypoints[npc.CurrentWaypointIndex])
			end
		end
	end

	print("[PatrolController] Updated waypoints for " .. #self._patrolNPCs .. " NPCs")
end

-- Lifecycle hooks
function PatrolController:OnStart()
	self:Init()
	self:Start()
end

return PatrolController
```

Here's the refactored code using an **object pool pattern** following Vertigo conventions. This eliminates per-frame allocations and improves performance significantly:

```luau
--!strict
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")

--!native
local vector_new = Vector3.new

local PoolTag = "ParticlePool"

-- Configuration
local MAX_PARTICLES = 50
local PARTICLE_SIZE = vector_new(0.5, 0.5, 0.5)
local PARTICLE_LIFETIME = 1

-- Pool state
local activeParticles: {Part} = {}
local inactiveParticles: {Part} = {}

-- Lifecycle state
local isRunning = false

local function init()
	-- Pre-create all particles in pool
	for i = 1, MAX_PARTICLES do
		local part = Instance.new("Part")
		part.Size = PARTICLE_SIZE
		part.Anchored = true
		part.CanCollide = false
		part.Material = EnumMaterial.Neon
		part.Transparency = 0.5
		CollectionService:AddTag(part, PoolTag)
		inactiveParticles[#inactiveParticles + 1] = part
	end

	isRunning = true
end

local function start()
	RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(updateParticles)
end

local function stop()
	isRunning = false
	-- Clean up pool on shutdown
	for _, part in ipairs(inactiveParticles) do
		part:Destroy()
	end
end

local function updateParticles(_dt: number)
	if not isRunning then return end

	-- Reuse inactive particles first, create new if pool exhausted
	for i = 1, MAX_PARTICLES do
		local part: Part?

		if #inactiveParticles > 0 then
			part = inactiveParticles[1]
			table.remove(inactiveParticles, 1)
		else
			break -- Pool exhausted, skip this iteration
		end

		if not part then continue end

		-- Update particle position using SIMD-friendly operations
		part.Position = vector_new(
			math.random(-100, 100),
			math.random(0, 50),
			math.random(-100, 100)
		)

		part.Parent = workspace
		activeParticles[#activeParticles + 1] = part

		-- Return to pool after lifetime
		task.delay(PARTICLE_LIFETIME, function()
			if part.Parent then
				part.Parent = nil
				inactiveParticles[#inactiveParticles + 1] = part
			end
		end)
	end
end

-- Export lifecycle functions for Vertigo module pattern
return {
	Init = init,
	Start = start,
	Stop = stop,
}
```

### Key Improvements:

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| **Per-frame allocations** | Pre-created pool of 50 Parts |
| **GC pressure** | No new instances after init |
| **Memory fragmentation** | Fixed-size pool prevents churn |
| **No lifecycle management** | Added Init/Start/Stop pattern |
| **Missing cleanup** | Proper teardown on stop |

### Usage:
```luau

ParticlePool.Init()
ParticlePool.Start()

-- Later, to stop:

```

- **Use ParticleEmitter** instead of Parts if visual effects are needed

```luau
--!strict
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")

-- Track active connections by instance for cleanup
local localConnections: { [BasePart]: RBXScriptConnection } = {}

-- @native for hot path performance
local function updateGlow(part: BasePart, dt: number)
    part.Transparency = 0.5 + math.sin(os.clock() * 3) * 0.3
end

local function setupGlow(part: BasePart)
    local connection = RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
        -- Validate part still exists and is tagged before updating
        if not CollectionService:HasTag(part, "GlowPart") then return end
        
        updateGlow(part, dt)
    end)
    
    localConnections[part] = connection
    
    -- Auto-cleanup if part is destroyed unexpectedly
    part.AncestryChanged:Connect(function()
        if not part.Parent then
            connection:Disconnect()
            localConnections[part] = nil
        end
    end)
end

local function cleanupGlow(part: BasePart)
    local connection = localConnections[part]
    if connection then
        connection:Disconnect()
        localConnections[part] = nil
    end
end

-- Connect to instance added signal
CollectionService:GetInstanceAddedSignal("GlowPart"):Connect(setupGlow)

-- Connect to instance removed signal for cleanup
CollectionService:GetInstanceRemovedSignal("GlowPart"):Connect(cleanupGlow)

-- Setup existing tagged instances
for _, part in CollectionService:GetTagged("GlowPart") do
    setupGlow(part)
end

-- Optional: Cleanup on script termination (for robustness)
game:GetService("RunService").Heartbeat:Connect(function() end) -- Keep script alive if needed
```

| **No removal handling** | Added `GetInstanceRemovedSignal` listener |

| **Performance** | `@native` on hot path, early exit if tag removed |

## Alternative: Use Debounce Pattern

For even better performance with many parts, consider batching updates or using a single Heartbeat connection that iterates all tagged parts:

```luau
local function updateAllGlowParts(dt: number)
    for part in CollectionService:GetTagged("GlowPart") do
        if part:IsA("BasePart") then
            updateGlow(part, dt)
        end
    end
end

local heartbeatConnection = RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(updateAllGlowParts)

-- Single connection, no per-instance overhead
```
